selected_important_turn.text: Ah, that doesn't work for me. I would need to have at least one water because one of mine has a leak (and I have an extra person joining me).
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn directly rebuffed the opponent's request for all the water and asserted a competing, non‑negotiable personal need. It locked the exchange into a bilateral claim/counterclaim over a scarce resource (water), raising the zero‑sum tension at a moment when the opponent had earlier shown willingness to concede food.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The reply is framed as a straight refusal and justification (leak + extra person), which escalates competition rather than opening tradespace. It misses an opportunity to convert the opponent's earlier generosity on food into a cooperative trade (e.g., acknowledging their constraints and proposing a split or trade that preserves their medical priority for firewood). The influence framing pushes for a slice rather than exploring package deals that could increase joint value.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Shift from a blunt rejection to a collaborative problem‑solving move: acknowledge their needs (firewood), thank them for offering food, then propose a concrete, small compromise on water (e.g., 'I need one water; could we split the rest 2/1 or trade one water for X?') or offer a trade that preserves their top priority while meeting your minimum. Signal flexibility and tradeoffs rather than an uncompromising claim.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — would open tradeoffs and allow creating a package that benefits both rather than a zero‑sum fight over water.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — by proposing explicit trades and acknowledging their medical priority, allocations would feel more balanced.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — collaborative phrasing and concrete trade options would lower resistance and speed agreement.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve — reduces adversarial tone, builds trust by showing willingness to accommodate their needs while stating yours.

value2reason.Low: I might need extra firewood if I decide to cool more or keep it lit longer.
value2reason.Medium: One of my water packages has a leak.
value2reason.High: One of my friends plans on joining me and they do not have their own food supply.

value2reason.Low: I'm on a diet, and don't need much food.
value2reason.High: I have a medical condition that causes my veins to close in the cold.

dialogue.text: I have a medical condition that causes my veins to close up when I get too cold. I will thus need the 3 firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: ☹️Oh, that sounds awful. Are you sure you need all three packages of firewood?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I might be able to get by with just two... I'm on a diet though, so I don't need as much food.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: That's a relief. I actually have a friend joining me and they don't have their own food supply. I don't have enough for two people.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: You can all the food if you need it.🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Thanks!🙂 Do you need any water packages?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Yes. Since the days are on the warmer side, I'm a afraid of getting dehydrated. I would love to have all of it...
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Ah, that doesn't work for me. I would need to have at least one water because one of mine has a leak (and I have an extra person joining me).
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate your offer for food, but I need at least one water package. Could we split the remaining water 2/1, or trade one water for a food package? I’m flexible but need to ensure my leaky water is addressed.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your need for water. Since I value firewood most, I’m willing to trade one food package for one water package. That way, we both secure what we need, and I can focus on getting the firewood I prioritize.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Your trade sounds fair. I’ll take one water and give you one food. Let’s split the remaining two waters 2/0 and take the firewood as is. That works for me.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Deal confirmed! I’ll take the two waters, one food, and all three firewoods. You get the remaining one food and one water. Safe camping!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
